description: Set up PWA infrastructure: manifest.json, service worker, app icons, and meta tags in layout.js for installability
steps: Create public/ directory at project root

Create public/manifest.json with: name 'CV Portal IJEF', short_name 'CV Portal', start_url '/', display 'standalone', theme_color '#2563EB' (blue-600 matching the app), background_color '#F9FAFB' (gray-50 bg), icons array referencing /icons/icon-192x192.png and /icons/icon-512x512.png
Create public/icons/ directory with SVG-based PNG icons (192x192 and 512x512) - use inline SVG with text 'CV' on blue background rendered to static SVG files that Next.js can serve
Create public/sw.js service worker with: install event (cache app shell), activate event (clean old caches), fetch event (network-first strategy with cache fallback for navigations)

Update src/app/layout.js metadata export to include: manifest link '/manifest.json', themeColor '#2563EB', appleWebApp meta (capable, status-bar-style, title), viewport meta for mobile, and icons. Also add a Script or head element to register the service worker on load
Since Next.js App Router uses metadata export for head tags, add the appropriate fields: metadataBase, manifest, themeColor, appleWebApp, viewport, icons, other (for mobile-web-app-capable)
Verify: run npm run build and confirm no errors
acceptance_criteria: public/manifest.json exists with correct app name, icons, display mode, and colors
public/icons/ contains icon-192x192.png (or .svg) and icon-512x512.png (or .svg) files
public/sw.js exists with basic offline caching strategy
src/app/layout.js includes PWA-related metadata (manifest link, theme-color, apple-mobile-web-app-capable)
npm run build completes without errors
verification: Run 'npm run build' in /projects/sandbox/cv-portal and confirm exit code 0
findings: In Next.js 15, themeColor must be in the viewport export (not metadata export) to avoid build warnings. Used SVG icons which are supported by modern browsers and avoid the need for image generation tools. Service worker uses network-first strategy suitable for a dynamic app. Build completes cleanly with zero warnings. Post-review fixes: bounded SW cache to static assets only, added controllerchange reload prompt, changed icon purpose to 'any' only, fixed statusBarStyle typo.
